key_insight: Thinner BaTiO3 membranes suppress visible cracking after release, but this crack resistance comes with increased nanoscale wrinkling, revealing a thickness-driven trade-off between fracture suppression and morphological stability.
engineering_relevance: The results provide practical thickness-selection guidelines for integrating freestanding ferroelectric oxide membranes into silicon-compatible low-power devices and memory platforms.
method: Varied BaTiO3 membrane thickness from 15 nm to 5 nm
Adjusted polymer support thickness during release
Used piezoresponse force microscopy to track post-release behavior
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that 5 nm BaTiO3 suppresses optically visible cracking while increasing dense nanoscale wrinkling, and that intermediate polymer thickness reduces wrinkle density in 10 nm membranes.
watch_item: Apparent ferroelectric performance can be confounded by wrinkling, charge screening, interfacial contact, and strain relaxation, so post-release morphology must be controlled before device claims.
paper_title: Thickness-dependent crack suppression and wrinkle formation in freestanding BaTiO3 membranes
